wasm.c(3) Library Functions Manual wasm.c(3)

NAME

wasm.c - WASM logging support for routing to browser console.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


EM_JS (void, js_console_log,(int level, const char *message), { const msg=UTF8ToString(message);const levelNames=['DEV', 'DEBUG', 'INFO', 'WARN', 'ERROR', 'FATAL'];const levelName=(level >=0 &&level<=5) ? levelNames[level] :'???^;const msgLen=msg.length;const hasTimestamp=msg.startsWith('[') &&msg.includes(':') &&msg.includes('.') &&msg[msg.indexOf(']')+1]===' ';const debugInfo=`[WASM_MSG_LEN=${msgLen}, HAS_TS=${hasTimestamp}]`;switch(level) { case 0:case 1:case 2:console.log(debugInfo, msg);break;case 3:console.warn(debugInfo, msg);break;case 4:case 5:console.error(debugInfo, msg);break;default:console.log(debugInfo, msg);} })
void platform_log_hook (log_level_t level, const char *message)
int wasm_parse_log_level (const uint8_t *buf, size_t count)
Parse log level from formatted message. void wasm_log_to_console (int fd, const uint8_t *buf, size_t count)
Route output to browser console based on log level.

Detailed Description

WASM logging support for routing to browser console.

Definition in file wasm.c.

Function Documentation

EM_JS (void , js_console_log , (int level, const char *message) , { const msg=UTF8ToString(message);const levelNames=['DEV', 'DEBUG', 'INFO', 'WARN', 'ERROR', 'FATAL'];const levelName=(level >=0 &&level<=5) ? levelNames[level] :'???^;const msgLen=msg.length;const hasTimestamp=msg.startsWith('[') &&msg.includes(':') &&msg.includes('.') &&msg[msg.indexOf(']')+1]===' ';const debugInfo=`[WASM_MSG_LEN=${msgLen}, HAS_TS=${hasTimestamp}]`;switch(level) { case 0:case 1:case 2:console.log(debugInfo, msg);break;case 3:console.warn(debugInfo, msg);break;case 4:case 5:console.error(debugInfo, msg);break;default:console.log(debugInfo, msg);} } )

void platform_log_hook (log_level_t level, const char * message)

Definition at line 51 of file wasm.c.

51                                                                {
52   if (message) {
53     // Strip ANSI codes for clean browser console output
54     static char clean_message[4096];
55     strip_ansi_codes(message, clean_message, sizeof(clean_message));
56     js_console_log((int)level, clean_message);
57   }
58 }








void
  wasm_log_to_console (int fd, const uint8_t * buf, size_t
  count)


Route output to browser console based on log level. Parses the log
    level from the message format [LEVEL] and routes to the appropriate console
    method (console.debug, console.log, etc).




Parameters


fd File descriptor (typically 1 for stdout, 2 for
  stderr)


buf Message buffer


count Message length


Definition at line 113 of file wasm.c.


113                                                                    {
114   // For WASM, route stdout/stderr to browser console
115   if ((fd == 1 || fd == 2) && buf && count > 0) {
116     // Parse log level from formatted message
117     int level = wasm_parse_log_level(buf, count);
118     if (level >= 0) {
119       // Create null-terminated string for console output
120       // Copy buffer to local array and null-terminate
121       static char msg_buffer[4096];
122       size_t copy_len = (count < sizeof(msg_buffer) - 1) ? count : (sizeof(msg_buffer) - 1);
123       memcpy(msg_buffer, buf, copy_len);
124       msg_buffer[copy_len] = '\0';
125 
126       // Remove trailing newline if present (console adds it automatically)
127       if (copy_len > 0 && msg_buffer[copy_len - 1] == '\n') {
128         msg_buffer[copy_len - 1] = '\0';
129       }
130 
131       // Route to console
132       js_console_log(level, msg_buffer);
133     }
134   }
135 }






References wasm_parse_log_level().






int
  wasm_parse_log_level (const uint8_t * buf, size_t count)


Parse log level from formatted message. Expects format:
    "[LEVEL] message..."




Parameters


buf Message buffer


count Message length


Returns


log_level_t enum value (0-5), or -1 if not
  found


Definition at line 62 of file wasm.c.


62                                                            {
63   if (!buf || count < 5) {
64     return -1; // Too short to contain [LEVEL]
65   }
66 
67   // Check if message starts with [
68   if (buf[0] != '[') {
69     return -1;
70   }
71 
72   // Look for closing ]
73   int bracket_end = -1;
74   for (size_t i = 1; i < count && i < 10; i++) {
75     if (buf[i] == ']') {
76       bracket_end = (int)i;
77       break;
78     }
79   }
80 
81   if (bracket_end < 3) {
82     return -1; // Too short, minimum is [XX]
83   }
84 
85   // Extract level string
86   int level_len = bracket_end - 1;
87   const char *level_start = (const char *)&buf[1];
88 
89   // Match against known level strings (from logging.c)
90   if (level_len == 3 && strncmp(level_start, "DEV", 3) == 0) {
91     return LOG_DEV;
92   }
93   if (level_len == 5 && strncmp(level_start, "DEBUG", 5) == 0) {
94     return LOG_DEBUG;
95   }
96   if (level_len == 4 && strncmp(level_start, "INFO", 4) == 0) {
97     return LOG_INFO;
98   }
99   if (level_len == 4 && strncmp(level_start, "WARN", 4) == 0) {
100     return LOG_WARN;
101   }
102   if (level_len == 5 && strncmp(level_start, "ERROR", 5) == 0) {
103     return LOG_ERROR;
104   }
105   if (level_len == 5 && strncmp(level_start, "FATAL", 5) == 0) {
106     return LOG_FATAL;
107   }
108 
109   return -1; // Level not recognized
110 }






References LOG_DEBUG, LOG_DEV, LOG_ERROR,
    LOG_FATAL, LOG_INFO, and LOG_WARN.


Referenced by wasm_log_to_console().
